# KAT fixtures

`paper_kat.json` holds the paper-blessed expected sizes, security bits,
and sign/verify cost models for the SPHINCS+ parameter sets that PQBC
uses, generated by running the authors'
[BlockstreamResearch/SPHINCS-Parameters](https://github.com/BlockstreamResearch/SPHINCS-Parameters)
sage scripts — `security.sage` (cryptographic bounds) + `costs.sage`
(sizes and costs).

`paper_kat_test.go` loads this fixture and cross-checks that our Go
types (`SPHINCSParams.SigSize()`, `PORSParams.TreeHeight()`, WOTS+C
`Ell`) agree with the oracle, modulo a single documented delta:

```
ours = paper + pors.CounterBytes()   (= paper + 4 at L1)
```

The 4-byte overhead is the "salt s" §10 Algorithm 1 explicitly returns
— PORS+FP's own grind salt, necessary for the verifier to reproduce the
(τ-fixed) inner index sampling. Sage's `compute_size` omits it from
`sig_size_bytes` (likely treating it as negligible vs the ~1.8 KB auth
set), but the construction itself requires it on the wire.

R size is 2N = 32 bytes per paper §11 / sage `randomness_size = 32`,
implemented by `Params.PRFmsgR` and matched by `SPHINCSParams.RSize()`.

## Regenerating

```bash
# From anywhere:
cd /tmp/SPHINCS-Parameters       # or wherever you clone the repo
sage /path/to/dump_katfixtures.sage
```

The script is not in the paper's repo upstream — it lives alongside the
scripts when regenerating. It uses the same `compute_signing_time`,
`compute_size`, and `compute_security` entry points as `costs.sage`
itself, so the fixture stays in lockstep with upstream.
